newsSince: ABB continued to advance data-center electrification: a partnership with VoltaGrid for stable AI data-center power and an NVIDIA 800V DC architecture collaboration (1MW rack support) reinforce its grid/power positioning. The SoftBank divestiture of ABB Robotics ($5.375B) remains on track to close mid-to-late 2026, with Robotics head Sami Atiya departing by end-2026 — structurally eroding ABB's robotics exposure. Q1 data-center electrification orders ($2.4B) already exceeded full-year 2025.

sources.label: Simply Wall St: ABB-NVIDIA tie-up / AI data center valuation
Futurum: ABB Q1 FY2026 driven by data center & grid demand
ABB Q1 FY2026 driven by data center & grid demand (Futurum)
ABB reshapes AI data center focus as robotics move nears SoftBank (Yahoo)
ABB to divest Robotics to SoftBank (ABB news)

verdicts.rankRationale: Passives/power lens: no new evidence since June 9. ABB stays a well-owned ~$198B benchmark name far from the passive/power-component bottleneck core; the +6.9% drift is price, not residual-alpha change. Hold near rank 58.
verdicts.bull: Triple-digit DC electrification order growth; raised 2026 outlook.
verdicts.neutral: Strong AI exposure but mega-cap and well-discovered; not the passive choke-point.
verdicts.bear: Crowded benchmark; little residual alpha vs passives at this size.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding/de-rating as a consensus AI-power name

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent lens: ABB is too large, too covered and too explicitly tagged to electrification/automation to be early alpha. No new evidence since June 9 changes that. Stays low-conviction at rank 95. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Deep electrification/grid backlog with AI-DC pull-through.
verdicts.neutral: Quality compounder but fully covered — opposite of latent.
verdicts.primaryRisk: No latency left — name is broadly held

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid/robotics lens: SoftBank divestiture (~$5.375B, closing 2026) is now firm and abandons the spin-off route — ABB's claim on a robotics-stack list weakens structurally as Robotics exits the consolidated entity. Down-drift continues, now on confirmed deal rather than rumor.
verdicts.bull: Robotics still consolidated until close; SoftBank ownership could re-energize the unit.
verdicts.neutral: Deal pending regulatory approval; ABB retains motion/automation exposure.
verdicts.bear: Post-close, ABB is no longer a robotics pure-play — minimal humanoid-stack relevance.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: None robotics-positive; deal removes the unit.

verdicts.rankRationale: Master list: electrification-bottleneck thesis (grid, switchgear, DC power) fully intact but unchanged since June 9 — Q1 strength was already known. +6.9% is price, not new evidence. Hold rank 18.
verdicts.bull: Triple-digit DC electrification orders; raised outlook; scarce power-bottleneck exposure.
verdicts.neutral: Strong but consensus AI-power name; valuation now reflects it.
verdicts.bear: Mega-cap crowding limits incremental bottleneck alpha.
verdicts.primaryRisk: De-rating if DC order growth normalizes
